revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] (crud category cleanup)
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Bunrei]][[es:Bunrei]][[fi:Bunrei]][[fr:Bunrei]][[id:Bunrei]][[ja:分霊]][[zh:分靈]]
{{short description|Division of Shinto kami spirit|qq=}} {{Nihongo|'''Bunrei''' or '''wakemitama'''|分霊|qq=}} is a [[Shinto]] technical term that indicates both the process of dividing a Shinto ''[[kami]]'' to be re-enshrined somewhere else (such as a house's ''[[kamidana]]''), and the spirit itself produced by the division.<ref>{{cite book |last1=Smyers |first1=Karen Ann |title=The Fox and the Jewel: Shared and Private Meanings in Contemporary Japanese Inari Worship |date=1999 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |location=Honolulu |isbn=9780824820589|qq=}}</ref> Shrines conduct ''bunrei'' to distribute them to "child" shrines elsewhere.<ref>{{cite web |title=「分霊」の意味や使い方 わかりやすく解説 Weblio辞書 |url=https://www.weblio.jp/content/%E5%88%86%E9%9C%8A |website=www.weblio.jp|lang=ja|qq=}}</ref> The spirit of ''kami'' does not decrease through this act, and a ''bunrei'' functions the same way as the original spirit.<ref>{{cite web |title=これで納得！ 同じ名前の神社がたくさんある理由 ～同じ名前の神様がたくさんいるの？～ – 早稲田神社 |url=https://wasedajinja.jp/2018/02/10/post-379/ |website=wasedajinja.jp|lang=ja|qq=}}</ref> The reason for conducting ''bunrei'' is often to make a ''kami'' more accessible to worshipers far from the main shrine. For details, see the article about the similar term [[Kanjō]]. ==References== {{Reflist|qq=}} {{Shinto-stub|qq=}} {{Shinto shrine|qq=}}
[[en:Bunrei]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[es:Bunrei]] [[fi:Bunrei]] [[fr:Bunrei]] [[id:Bunrei]] [[ja:分霊]] [[zh:分靈]]
{{wikidata link|Q195793}} [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Taoism]]
